# Primary sources

Recheck examples against the target repository's toolchain and compatibility policy at execution
time. When network access is unavailable, report the freshness gap.

- [The Rust Book: structs](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch05-00-structs.html) — grouping related values and defining methods.
- [The Rust Book: enums and pattern matching](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch06-00-enums.html) — data-carrying variants and exhaustive handling.
- [The Rust Reference: functions](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/items/functions.html) — function item syntax and behavior.
- [The Rust Reference: implementations](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/items/implementations.html) — inherent and trait implementations and associated items.
- [The Rust Reference: type aliases](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/items/type-aliases.html) — aliases as names for existing types and associated types.
- [The Rust API Guidelines](https://rust-lang.github.io/api-guidelines/) — explicitly non-mandatory API design recommendations.
- [Rust API Guidelines checklist](https://rust-lang.github.io/api-guidelines/checklist.html) — methods with clear receivers, constructors, newtypes, custom argument types, and builders.
- [Serde attributes](https://serde.rs/attributes.html) — representation and compatibility-sensitive serialization controls.
- [Agent Skills specification](https://agentskills.io/specification) — portable skill structure and progressive disclosure.
- [Codex skills](https://developers.openai.com/codex/build-skills) — current Codex discovery and authoring guidance.
- [Claude Code skills](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills) — current Claude Code skill behavior and format.
- [OpenCode skills](https://opencode.ai/docs/skills/) — current OpenCode skill discovery and compatibility guidance.
